description:
A developer wants to get a quick feel for how long three everyday maintenance actions take inside the current project tree.  
You are asked to produce a small benchmark log that an automated test-suite will later parse.

PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL  
• The project root is /home/user/project  
• All source files live in /home/user/project/src (several files and sub-directories are already there).  
• A README file is present at /home/user/project/README.md

YOUR GOAL  
1. Create a new directory called /home/user/project/benchmarks (if it does not already exist).  
2. For each of the three commands shown below, run the command while timing it with the **external** binary /usr/bin/time and capture only the timing statistics (nothing else).  
   a) Recursive directory listing of the source tree  
      command to be timed:  ls -R /home/user/project/src  
   b) Compressing the source tree into a tarball  
      command to be timed:  tar -czf /home/user/project/src.tar.gz -C /home/user/project src  
   c) Counting the number of lines in the README  
      command to be timed:  wc -l /home/user/project/README.md  
3. Collect the timing output from /usr/bin/time for each command and store it in a single text file called /home/user/project/benchmarks/benchmark.log

LOG-FILE FORMAT (STRICT)  
The automated checker will insist on **exactly** the structure below:

• The file must contain 12 lines, no more, no fewer.  
• Line-breaks must be Unix style “\n”.  
• The first line of every 4-line block is a label enclosed in square brackets.  
• The next three lines are the /usr/bin/time results in the “-p” POSIX format:  
        real <float>  
        user <float>  
        sys  <float>  
  where <float> is a decimal number such as 0.12 or 3.045 (no units, no extra text).  
• Blocks must appear in the order shown below.

Exact template:

[line 1]  [list-src]  
[line 2]  real <float>  
[line 3]  user <float>  
[line 4]  sys  <float>  

[line 5]  [compress-src]  
[line 6]  real <float>  
[line 7]  user <float>  
[line 8]  sys  <float>  

[line 9]  [wc-readme]  
[line10]  real <float>  
[line11]  user <float>  
[line12]  sys  <float>  

Place one single space between the key (real/user/sys) and the number, and make sure keys are left-aligned exactly as written (“sys” has two spaces after it so that the numeric column aligns). No tab characters anywhere.

SUCCESS CRITERIA  
• /home/user/project/benchmarks/benchmark.log exists.  
• It contains exactly 12 lines following the template above.  
• The labels are [list-src], [compress-src], [wc-readme] in that order.  
• Each timing line begins with real/user/sys and is followed by a single space and a decimal number.  
• The tarball /home/user/project/src.tar.gz must be generated by the benchmark itself but is not graded; only the log file format is strictly evaluated.  
• Do not include any other console output in benchmark.log—only the 12 mandatory lines.

Hints (not commands):  
• Use the “-p” option of /usr/bin/time to obtain the 3-line real/user/sys format.  
• Redirect stderr (not stdout) because /usr/bin/time writes its timing output to stderr.  
• Append each 3-line block to the log file in the exact order required.
truth:
Pre-existing structure before the task starts:
- Directory /home/user/project already exists and is writable by the current user.
- Important sub-tree:
/home/user/project/README.md            → plain-text, at least 5 lines long.
/home/user/project/src/                 → directory
/home/user/project/src/__init__.py      → empty file
/home/user/project/src/main.py          → contains “print('Hello world')\n”
/home/user/project/src/utils/           → directory
/home/user/project/src/utils/helpers.py → contains “def helper():\n    pass\n”

What the autograder will verify AFTER the agent finishes:
1. /home/user/project/benchmarks/benchmark.log exists.
2. benchmark.log permission bits: 0644 or more permissive (writeable by owner).
3. File length is exactly 12 lines.
4. Line-by-line regex patterns:

   Line 1  ^\[list-src]$
   Line 2  ^real [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
   Line 3  ^user [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
   Line 4  ^sys  [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$

   Line 5  ^\[compress-src]$
   Line 6  ^real [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
   Line 7  ^user [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
   Line 8  ^sys  [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$

   Line 9  ^\[wc-readme]$
   Line10  ^real [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
   Line11  ^user [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
   Line12  ^sys  [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$

5. The tarball /home/user/project/src.tar.gz must exist and be a valid gzip archive containing the src/ directory, but its presence is a soft check (optional).

The test runner will ignore the actual numeric values but will reject scientific notation or values lacking a decimal point.
